statement: The Earth is flat.
explanation: The Earth is actually an oblate spheroid - slightly flattened at the poles and bulging at the equator due to its rotation. This has been proven by satellite imagery, circumnavigation, and countless scientific measurements!

statement: Water boils at 100°C at sea level.
explanation: At standard atmospheric pressure (sea level), water boils at exactly 100°C (212°F). However, at higher altitudes where air pressure is lower, water boils at lower temperatures - which is why cooking takes longer in the mountains!

statement: The Great Wall of China is visible from space.
explanation: This is a common myth! Astronauts have confirmed that the Great Wall is virtually invisible from low Earth orbit without magnification. It's only about 6 meters wide and blends in with the natural landscape. However, highways and airports are more visible!

statement: Sharks are mammals.
explanation: Sharks are fish, not mammals! They're cartilaginous fish (their skeletons are made of cartilage, not bone), they breathe through gills, and they're cold-blooded. Mammals like whales and dolphins breathe air and are warm-blooded.

statement: A tomato is technically a fruit.
explanation: Botanically speaking, tomatoes are fruits because they develop from the ovary of a flower and contain seeds. However, in 1893, the U.S. Supreme Court ruled them vegetables for trade purposes! Cucumbers, peppers, and pumpkins are also technically fruits.

statement: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
explanation: Lightning definitely strikes the same place multiple times! Tall structures like the Empire State Building get struck about 20-25 times per year. In fact, lightning tends to strike the highest point in an area repeatedly.

statement: Humans have more than 5 senses.
explanation: Beyond the traditional five (sight, hearing, smell, taste, touch), we have many more! These include proprioception (body position), equilibrioception (balance), thermoception (temperature), and nociception (pain). Scientists estimate we have between 9 and 21 different senses!

statement: Goldfish have a 3-second memory.
explanation: Goldfish actually have impressive memories! Studies show they can remember things for at least three months and can be trained to recognize shapes, colors, and sounds. They can even tell time and anticipate feeding schedules!

statement: Mount Everest is the tallest mountain on Earth.
explanation: At 8,849 meters (29,032 feet) above sea level, Mount Everest is Earth's highest point. However, if measured from base to peak, Mauna Kea in Hawaii is taller (10,210m total, but most is underwater). And from Earth's center, Ecuador's Mount Chimborazo is furthest!

statement: Penguins can fly.
explanation: Penguins cannot fly in the air - they evolved to swim instead! However, they're excellent 'flyers' underwater, using their wings as flippers to 'fly' through the ocean at speeds up to 22 mph. Their dense bones help them dive deep.

statement: The sun is a star.
explanation: The Sun is a medium-sized yellow dwarf star, about 4.6 billion years old! It's one of over 100 billion stars in our Milky Way galaxy. It provides Earth with light and heat through nuclear fusion, converting 600 million tons of hydrogen into helium every second.

statement: Bats are blind.
explanation: Bats are not blind! All bats can see, and many species have excellent vision. While they do use echolocation (biological sonar) to navigate in darkness, fruit bats rely heavily on their eyesight and have vision comparable to humans.

statement: Octopuses have three hearts.
explanation: Octopuses have three hearts: two pump blood to the gills, while the third pumps it to the rest of the body. Interestingly, the heart that delivers blood to the body stops beating when the octopus swims - which is why they prefer crawling!

statement: The human body has four lungs.
explanation: Humans have two lungs, not four! The right lung is larger and divided into three lobes, while the left lung has two lobes to make room for the heart. Together, they contain about 2,400 km of airways and 300-500 million alveoli.

statement: Honey never spoils.
explanation: Honey is virtually immortal! Archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still edible. Its low moisture content and acidic pH prevent bacterial growth. Bees add an enzyme that produces hydrogen peroxide, giving honey antibacterial properties.

statement: A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
explanation: Venus takes 243 Earth days to rotate once on its axis (one Venus day), but only 225 Earth days to orbit the Sun (one Venus year). This means a day on Venus is longer than its year! Venus also rotates backwards compared to most planets.

statement: Chameleons change color to blend in with their environment.
explanation: Chameleons primarily change color to communicate mood, regulate temperature, and attract mates - not for camouflage! They use special cells called chromatophores containing different pigments. Mood and temperature changes trigger these cells to expand or contract, creating color shifts.

statement: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean.
explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers about 165 million square kilometers - larger than all of Earth's land area combined! It contains more than half of the world's ocean water and is so vast you could fit all the continents inside it with room to spare.

statement: Bulls are enraged by the color red.
explanation: Bulls are colorblind to red and green! They actually react to the movement of the matador's cape, not its color. In bullfighting, the red color is traditional and helps hide bloodstains, but the bull would charge at any color of moving fabric.

statement: Bananas are berries, but strawberries aren't.
explanation: Botanically, berries must develop from one flower with one ovary and have seeds embedded in the flesh. Bananas qualify, but strawberries don't - they're 'accessory fruits' with seeds on the outside! Blueberries and grapes are true berries, while raspberries and blackberries aren't.

statement: The Eiffel Tower can be 15 cm taller during summer.
explanation: Due to thermal expansion, the iron in the Eiffel Tower expands when heated by the sun, making it grow up to 15 cm (6 inches) taller in summer! The side facing the sun also curves away from it by up to 18 cm as it expands more than the shaded side.

statement: Humans can breathe and swallow at the same time.
explanation: Adults cannot breathe and swallow simultaneously - the epiglottis blocks the windpipe when swallowing to prevent choking! However, babies can do both at once until about 6-9 months old, which allows them to breathe while feeding. We lose this ability as our larynx descends.

statement: Your fingernails grow faster than your toenails.
explanation: Fingernails grow about 3-4 mm per month, while toenails only grow 1-2 mm per month - roughly 3 times slower! The reason is likely related to better blood circulation in fingers and more frequent use. Interestingly, nails grow faster in summer and on your dominant hand!

statement: All planets in our solar system rotate counterclockwise.
explanation: Venus and Uranus are the rebels! Venus rotates clockwise (retrograde rotation), possibly due to a massive ancient collision. Uranus rotates on its side at a 98-degree angle, likely knocked over by an impact. All other planets rotate counterclockwise when viewed from above the North Pole.

statement: The Amazon rainforest produces 20% of the world's oxygen.
explanation: The Amazon rainforest produces about 20% of Earth's oxygen through photosynthesis, earning it the nickname 'the lungs of the Earth.' However, it also consumes most of that oxygen through decomposition and respiration. The ocean's phytoplankton actually produces more net oxygen!

statement: Diamonds are made from compressed coal.
explanation: Diamonds are not formed from coal! They crystallize from carbon under extreme heat and pressure 140-190 km beneath Earth's surface, long before coal even formed. Most diamonds are 1-3 billion years old, while coal formed from plants only 300 million years ago. The carbon sources are completely different!

statement: An ostrich's eye is bigger than its brain.
explanation: An ostrich's eye measures about 5 cm (2 inches) in diameter - the largest eye of any land animal - while its brain is only about the size of a walnut! Despite their small brains, ostriches are actually quite clever and can reach speeds of 70 km/h (43 mph).

statement: Humans only use 10% of their brains.
explanation: This is a complete myth! Brain imaging studies show we use virtually all parts of our brain, just not all at once. Even during sleep, the brain is active. The myth may have originated from early neuroscience misunderstandings. We use 100% of our brain - just different areas for different tasks!

statement: A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'.
explanation: A group of flamingos is indeed called a 'flamboyance'! Other fun collective nouns include: a 'murder' of crows, a 'parliament' of owls, a 'conspiracy' of ravens, and a 'bloat' of hippos. Flamingos get their pink color from carotenoids in their diet of algae and shrimp!

statement: The Moon has its own light.
explanation: The Moon doesn't produce its own light - it reflects sunlight! The bright 'moonlight' we see is actually the Sun's light bouncing off the Moon's rocky surface. This is why the Moon appears to go through phases: we're seeing different amounts of its sunlit side from Earth.
